channel.title: dotnet/interactive Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/interactive, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: [VSCode] On language change, re-queue diagnostics - @brettfo
item.description:
REPRO:

  1. Create a new C# notebook.
  2. Set the cell contents to [1;2;3;4] |> List.sum
  3. Wait for the C# diagnostics to appear; 'Identifier expected'.
  4. Change the cell language to F#.

EXPECTED: The diagnostics should disappear because they're no longer valid.

RESULT: The stale C# diagnostics are still present until the cell is changed by typing.

item.title: Issue: Add "run" magic command to run other notebooks - @marckruzik
item.description:
Describe the solution you'd like Jupyter Notebooks have the "run" magic command, which allows to run another notebook from any cell. https://ipython.readthedocs.io/en/stable/interactive/magics.html#magic-run

This is really useful to:

  • store your common methods
  • list requirements (nuggets, etc)
  • do all data acquisition
  • separate a big notebook into several smaller notebooks (so the main notebook can call the smaller ones, or you can "chain" them with notebook2 calling notebook1)
  • work as a team with each member working on its own notebook

Describe alternatives you've considered People have been asking to associate a notebook with a project: https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/issues/890 But it would force you to work with a project, so you would still not be able to work on several notebooks at the same time.

It's possible to load some cs code by creating a dll with PowerShell and dotnet: https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/issues/192 But it's not reading code from notebooks.

Those solutions could work to store classes with common methods. But the run command would be able to interactively execute code in cells, staying true to a 100% interactive notebook experience.

item.title: Issue: nteract filters do not capture cells shortcut (a, d, etc) - @marckruzik
item.description:
Describe the bug

I am running the basic example with nteract. https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/blob/main/samples/ExtensionLab/ExtensionLab-nteract.ipynb

In the nteract output, when I am trying to use the filter, it writes down the keys I am typing, but the notebook captures those keys as well as shortcuts. So when I am typing a key like "a", it is both written in the filter and it activates the notebook shortcut ("a" = create a new cell). Same goes for other shorcuts and shortcuts combinations, such as "d d".

Here is my exact code:

#i "nuget:https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/dnceng/public/_packaging/dotnet5/nuget/v3/index.json"
#i "nuget:https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/dnceng/public/_packaging/dotnet-tools/nuget/v3/index.json"

#r "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.ExtensionLab,*-*"

 var data = new[]
            {
                new {Type="orange", Price=1.2},
                new {Type="apple" , Price=1.3},
                new {Type="grape" , Price=1.4}
            };

data

data.ExploreWithNteract().Display();

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using?

Version: 1.0.235701+3881a96164de75fca84f5f11027f3606b7878044 Build date: 2021-07-18T15:05:14.4777930Z

item.title: Issue: .dib is already a registered file extension in Windows - @dennisl68-castra
item.description:
Describe the bug

Double clicking on a .dib file has Windows 10 to try to open it the Photos app.

Screenshots

item.title: Issue: Allow `/openNotebook` URI handler to better handle notebook types. - @brettfo
item.description:
Currently the /openNotebook URI handler simply looks at the end of the URI for either .dib or .ipynb to determine how to open the notebook.

A better solution would probably be this order:

  1. If the URI query parameters contains as=.dib or as=.ipynb, use that. This is similar to how the /newNotebook URI handler works.
  2. If (1) didn't produce anything, read the mime type from the response object; something like application/x-notebook-dib or application/x-notebook-ipynb, etc.
  3. If (1) and (2) didn't produce anything, then fall back to the current behavior of looking at the end of the URI.

item.title: Issue: Info about Type for formatting - @WhiteBlackGoose
item.description:
This is really not helpful: Not only because this particularly one gives a 404 , but mainly because I want to see its properties here, not somewhere. But link is useful, so my idea is to output both the link AND info about the type the same way we get it for other objects.

@jonsequitur assign this one to me, if you have no objectives.

item.title: Issue: Python extension required although only using PowerShell when creating a new .ipynb - @dennisl68-castra
item.description:
Describe the bug

When creating a new Notebook for use with PowerShell, an error is displayed requiring Python. The error can be ignored.

  1. Ctrl-Shift-P
  2. .Net Interactive: Create new blank notebook
  3. Create as .ipynb
  4. PowerShell

The kernel is correclty set to .Net Interactive

Using the .dib extension doesn't produce the error message.

Please complete the following:

.NET Interactive v1.0.2357010 Jupyter v2021.8.1013163132 VS Code

Version: 1.58.0 (system setup)
Commit: 2d23c42a936db1c7b3b06f918cde29561cc47cd6

Chrome: 89.0.4389.128
Node.js: 14.16.0
V8: 8.9.255.25-electron.0

item.title: Issue: #!value --from-url calls the URL when there is also other content in the cell - @jonsequitur
item.description:
This test illustrates the issue:

        [Fact]
        public async Task when_from_url_is_used_with_content_then_the_response_is_not_stored()
        {
            using var kernel = CreateKernel();

            var url = $"http://example.com/{Guid.NewGuid():N}";
            var result = await kernel.SubmitCodeAsync($@"
#!value --name hi --from-url {url}
// some content");

            result.KernelEvents.ToSubscribedList();

            kernel
                .FindKernel("value")
                .As<DotNetKernel>()
                .GetVariableNames()
                .Should()
                .NotContain("hi");
        }

item.title: Issue: Exchange Online Compatibility? - @vijaidial
item.description:
Describe the bug

I'm attempting to use the Exchange Online Mangement module and it keeps crashing. The cmdlet successfully connects but soon after a Powershell exception is thrown.

The command I'm using is

connect-ExchangeOnline -userprinciplename <USERNAME>

The error message is:

Exception: C:\Program Files\WindowsPowerShell\Modules\ExchangeOnlineManagement\2.0.5\netCore\ExchangeOnlineManagement.psm1:475
Line |
 475 |  … PSSession = New-ExoPSSession -ExchangeEnvironmentName $ExchangeEnviro …
     |                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
     | An error has occurred which PowerShell cannot handle. A remote session might have
     | ended.

I am able to use this command successfully in the windows powershell console; the error is only thrown in Jupyter and in VSCode when using .NET Interactive

Is this package compatible with Exchange Online?

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command.
  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version.

Version: 1.0.235701+3881a96164de75fca84f5f11027f3606b7878044 Build date: 2021-07-09T01:12:29.8185285Z

  • OS

item.title: Issue: Prepare .NET 6 update - @jonsequitur
Issue: Move JavaScript kernels into VS Code extension - @jonsequitur
Issue: Proxy from VS Code host JavaScript kernel to Web view JavaScript kernel - @jonsequitur
Issue: Create a diagnostic notebook for user bug investigations - @jonsequitur
item.description:
We need to talk about what this does but I am currently thinking that we need a magic command that turns on / off the logging for components and perhaps the display of additional information.

For example - #r "nuget: ... blah " can produce an msbuild binary log file which significantly improves our ability to figure out why #r "nuget: " commands fail.

  • [ ] Implement diagnostic #!diag command args Details: Wire up basic diag command
  • [ ] Implement: Command: #!diag location Details: Display the diagnostics location
  • [ ] Implement: Command: #!diag logs path Details: Specify a target location for the diagnostic session artifacts
  • [ ] Implement: Command: #!diag nuget full Details: Produces binlog and copies all #r nuget artifacts to the location specified in log_path
  • [ ] Implement:
    Command: #!diag nuget display resolutions Details: Displays all resolved assemblies on screen
  • [ ] Implement: Command: #!diag nuget display sources Details: Displays all specified nuget sources

Obviously I have spent more time diagnosing nuget issues, so I know what I want to see there, please add suggestions for other items that need diagnostics added and the type of information that would be usefull.

Another consideration, I have no opinion of ... 'well I do actually but I don't want to pollute the well' ... should the log information, such as log files location, nuget binlog file name, project file name be programmatically accessible as a value.

item.title: Issue: Unable to install nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer - @DebtheDBA
item.description:
Describe the bug

I installed VS Code so I could learn how to set up .NET Interactive notebooks. I used the System Installer instead of the User Installer.

I installed dotnet-sdk-5.0.301-win-x64.exe as well as dotnet-runtime-5.0.7-win-x64.exe I installed these extensions in VS Code, if this helps with set up issues: NuGet Package Manager (1.1.6), .NET Interactive Notebooks (1.0.2351010), SQL Server (mssql) (1.10.1), Jupyter (2021.6.999662501)

When creating a .NET Notebook, as a jpynb file, I added a C# code cell and ran: #r "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer,-"

I got the following error: Error: C:\Users\Deborah\AppData\Local\Temp\nuget\6260--159f7760-6286-4970-bea7-77ad5cb1ce35\Project.fsproj : error NU1100: Unable to resolve 'Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer (>= 0.0.0-0)' for 'net5.0'. C:\Users\Deborah\AppData\Local\Temp\nuget\6260--159f7760-6286-4970-bea7-77ad5cb1ce35\Project.fsproj : error NU1100: Unable to resolve 'Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer (>= 0.0.0-0)' for 'net5.0/win-x64'.

When researching and reaching out for general help with the problem, it sounded like there may be a config setting with nuGet and it was recommended that I open an issue.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command. (see below for screenshot) .NET Interactive © 2020 Microsoft Corporation Version: 1.0.233002+bb8147548dcdc9c83212e3b3631aa2e0148a0547 Build date: 2021-07-05T23:00:52.1339606Z https://github.com/dotnet/interactive

  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version.

  • OS
    • [X ] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [ ] Chrome
    • [ ] Edge

Screenshots

item.title: Issue: Disable run for .dib files when in untrusted workspaces - @jonsequitur
Issue: July 2021 rough plan of work - @jonsequitur
Issue: Ansi output not supported - @DonJayamanne
item.description:
The following cell doesn't work in Notebooks:

Console.Write("\x1b[38;2;0;255;0m"); // set text color to green
Console.WriteLine("green");
Console.Write("\x1b[0m"); // reset colors

Basically any ANSI codes are not supported (because all output is treated as as plain text).

item.title: Issue: nuget loading messages are very invasive - @colombod
item.description:

Loading packages or connecting to mssql produces a very long and verbose output, also a lot of package names are repeated.

item.title: Issue: Uninstallation problem - @WilliamQue
item.description:
Describe the bug

After testing and uninstalling .net interactive notebooks in vscode, I reopened ipynb file from vscode explorer, the file was still opened by the uninstalled extension, rather than jupyter notebook once.

How to uninstall .net interactive notebooks completely?

Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

item.title: Issue: Clarify, or heighten visibility, regarding port-ranges for #!js magic to work - @devhell
item.description:
Describe the bug

My colleagues and I tried to execute javascript magic on our JupyterHub instance run on AKS, only to run into an HTTP Endpoint timeout error (see below screenshot). It was not clear at all to us why this occurred. We double-checked with local JupyterHub docker containers and had the same issues there, as well as the example Binder instance linked to from here.

Another colleague had JupyterLab running natively on their Windows machine and had no issues. A very productive discussion with @colombod led to the solution, which is that the extra port-ranges need to be re-defined. On Linux, port-ranges below 1024 are privileged. I built the docker container @colombod referred to here with port-range 9000-9200 and tested javascript successfully.

I think it would be helpful to raise the visibility of the port requirements, especially since the Binder instances will fail executing javascript magic and people might assume that they are "reference instances".

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

Version: 1.0.232501+a6b7f7626e5c0a4e3854799cca9f97eeb519904e Build date: 2021-06-25T19:40:04.0000000Z

  • OS
    • [x] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [x] Linux (NixOS)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [x] Chrome
    • [x] Edge
    • [x] Firefox
    • [ ] Safari
  • Frontend
    • [ ] Jupyter Notebook
    • [x] Jupyter Lab
    • [ ] nteract
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code

item.title: Issue: F# Code with long string output caused display problem - @ingted
item.description:
Describe the bug

#r @"nuget:FSharp.Data"
#r @"nuget:Http.fs"
#r "System.Xml.Linq.dll"
open FSharp.Data
open Hopac
open HttpFs.Client

let DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRate =
    Request.createUrl Get "https://data.treasury.gov/feed.svc/DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRateData"
    |> Request.responseAsString
    |> run

let DailyTreasuryYieldCurveRate =
    Request.createUrl Get "https://data.treasury.gov/feed.svc/DailyTreasuryYieldCurveRateData"
        |> Request.responseAsString
        |> run

type DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRateType = XmlProvider<Schema="G:\\DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRateData.xsd">

let DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRateData =
    DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRateType.Parse DailyTreasuryRealYieldCurveRate

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? v1.0.2309030

  • OS
    • Windows Server 2019
  • Browser
    • Chrome
  • Frontend
    • Visual Studio Code

item.title: Issue: When opening a previously save .ipynb the cell results are blank? - @iancoetzer
item.description:
Describe the bug

Hi, when I create a new .NET interactive .ipynb workbook that returns some records from a SQL database table the results disappear when I save and re-open the workbook.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out: 1.0.230903

  • OS
    • [ X ] Windows 10
  • Frontend
    • [ X ] Visual Studio Code

Screenshots

image 1

image 2

item.title: Issue: Stable versions not available at Visual Studio Marketplace for manual download - @dennisl68-castra
item.description:
The version of the extension you tried to install is only for insiders. The latest package that'll install on stable is 1.0.2309030.

If it helps in the future, all of our extension version numbers look like 1.0.xxxxxx0 or 1.0.xxxxxx1; the ones that end in 0 are for stable VS Code, the ones that end in 1 are for Insiders.

Originally posted by @brettfo in https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/issues/1458#issuecomment-871647063

item.title: Issue: Can't install extension using .vsix offline - @dennisl68-castra
item.description:
Describe the bug

When trying to install ms-dotnettools.dotnet-interactive-vscode-1.0.2325011.vsix using Install from VSIX..., I get the error

Unable to install extension 'ms-dotnettools.dotnet-interactive-vscode' as it is not compatible with VS Code '1.57.1'.

Trying to install the Jupyter Notebook extension gives the same error.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command. N/A
  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version. N/A

item.title: Issue: Improve API around notebook parsing and serialization - @brettfo
item.description: The APIs provided by NotebookFileFormatHandler.cs for parsing and serializing notebooks need some improvements to make them easier to use, e.g., explicitly specify format instead of inferring based on file extension, allow reading from/writing to a file path instead of only byte arrays, etc.

item.title: Issue: Cells that get stuck running can sometimes not be stopped. - @Treit
item.description:
Describe the bug

Cells that get stuck running can sometimes not be stopped by clicking the 'Stop cell' button.

I have seen multiple times a cell that gets stuck in a running state and clicking the little square 'stop cell' button does nothing.

I recently saw this with the following trivial notebook:

#!csharp

int i = 1234;

#!javascript

let x = 0
interactive.csharp.getVariable('i').then(v => console.log(v))
console.log(x)

#!markdown

<h1>Test!</h1>
Test test test

For some reason this showed several cells that were 'stuck' running and clicking the stop button did nothing.

.NET Interactive Version

Version: 1.0.230903+1a272ef3782d6e88b6d36ebd648fb1882da0a530 Build date: 2021-06-28T20:51:38.7685995Z

  • OS
    • [x] Windows 11 (build 22000.51)
    • [ ] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)

    • [ ] Jupyter Lab

item.title: Issue: Cell runs forever when using interactive.csharp.getVariable in conjunction with console.log in a javascript cell - @Treit
item.description:
Describe the bug

Creating the following .dib file and executing the cells (usually) causes the second cell to run indefinitely:

#!csharp

int i = 1234;

#!javascript

let x = 0
interactive.csharp.getVariable('i').then(v => console.log(v))
console.log(x)

.NET Interactive Version

Neither #!about or dotnet interactive --version seemed to work for me, but the vscode extension says it's the following version: v1.0.2309030

item.title: Issue: How to increase the 3 minute timeout to package dependencies? - @GeorgeS2019
item.description:
New to .NET interactive!

Is there a way to increase the 3 min timeout leading to

Error: System.TimeoutException: Package restore took longer than expected for packages: libtorch-cpu.

item.title: Issue: Are polygot notebooks supported in jupyter/mybinder? - @JonTheTurnip
item.description:
Hi there,

Hopefully a very quick question.

I thought I'd try creating a notebook that includes both c# and f# cells, and then publish this to binder using the dockerfile from this repo.

It all works well for the c# cells but not for the f# cells. It makes me think that perhaps polygots are not yet supported in jupyter/mybinder?

Apologies if this is an obvious question, but it's all new to me. If needed, my repo is here...

https://github.com/JonTheTurnip/dot-net-notebooks

I also tried nbviewer with a dotnet interactive notebook and that did not work, assume that's expected behaviour at this time?

Cheers

Jon

item.title: Issue: Add support for console.table - @jonsequitur
item.description: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Console/table

item.title: Issue: Configurable garbage collection settings - @nhirschey
item.description:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. I understand that this will not be high priority, but long term it would be nice if there was a way to configure the garbage collection setting of the dotnet runtime used by a notebook.

One of .NET's strengths for data analysis is easily parallelized code. After switching the runtime from workstation garbage collection to server garbage collection, I have often seen close to linear (in CPU threads) performance improvements from code using FSharp.Array.Parallel and PLinq (see related issue https://github.com/dotnet/fsharp/issues/5502).

I could write smarter code that does fewer allocations, but it'd be nice to take advantage of a simple GC switch to get dramatically better performance from idiomatic code.

Describe the solution you'd like

  1. I'd like a configurable way to say "run this notebook with .NET's server garbage collection setting enabled." I think this is important for C# and F# notebooks.

    Is it possible to add something to notebook metadata indicating to load the kernel with server GC enabled? In FSharp, this would be changing fsi.runtimeconfig.json to

{
  "runtimeOptions": {
    "tfm": "net5.0",
    "framework": {
      "name": "Microsoft.NETCore.App",
      "version": "5.0.5"
    },
    "configProperties": {
      "System.GC.Server": true
   }
  }
}
  1. Greater than 32 core machines are getting more common. To enable .net interactive kernels to use > 32 cores (>64 threads) on windows, you need to change the GC setting to allow it to span multiple CPU groups

item.title: Issue: Restarting kernel doesn't reset JavaScript state - @jonsequitur
item.description:
Repro steps:

  1. Set a variable in a JavaScript cell:
myVariable = 123;

  1. Restart the kernel

  2. In another JavaScript cell, run this:

console.log(myVariable);

Expected:

Error: ReferenceError: myVariable is not defined

Actual:

123

item.title: Issue: No renderer could be found for MIME type: application/json - @jonsequitur
item.description: When calling console.log with a value other than a string in VS Code Insiders, the error No renderer could be found for MIME type: application/json is shown.

item.title: Issue: README link broken --> Install .NET Interactive to create and run .NET notebooks on your machine. - @fortunewalla
item.description:
The below link from README is broken:

Install .NET Interactive to create and run .NET notebooks on your machine.

https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/blob/main/docs/NotebooksLocalExperience.md

item.title: Issue: d3js.ipynb is still broken on mybinder - @RandomFractals
item.description:
Tried your polyglot d3js.ipynb sample notebook on mybinder.

I still get this error. In fact, I've never seen that sample notebook work there:

item.title: Issue: [EXTERNAL] Start-Transcript in Powershell .NET Interactive notebook hides future output - @gauravjag
item.description:
PowerShellTranscriptFailure.zip

Once Start-Transcript appears on a PowerShell notebook, any attempt to output to the notebook fails. See attached notebook for a repro.

item.title: Issue: Differentiate deserialization APIs for .dib versus .ipynb - @jonsequitur
item.description: Currently the file format is inferred based on the file extension. This should be made explicit.

item.title: Issue: Work with .NET Framework V4.8 - @dubjap
item.description:
The package and version I'm asking about:

Version: 1.0.230903+1a272ef3782d6e88b6d36ebd648fb1882da0a530

Question

Is it possible to have the .net interactive notebook in VS Code running against the .NET framework V4.8? A certain program is providing an API by means of a DLL that is written in .NET framework. It works in the C# interactive in VS but would be really cool to have it in a notebook.

item.title: Issue: Error when running "#r "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer,*-*"" in F# cell - @JCarnall
item.description:
Hi there

I'm trying out the code snippets in Maria's blog here

When I run "#r "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer,-"" in an F# cell I get the following error

I've tried the same thing in a C# cell and there is no error, but the next step does not display the expected results of the help command

Version of .Net Interactive is 1.0.225602

  • OS
    • [x ] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [ x] Chrome
    • [ ] Edge
    • [ ] Firefox
    • [ ] Safari
  • Frontend
    • [ ] Jupyter Notebook
    • [ ] Jupyter Lab
    • [ ] nteract
    • [ x] Visual Studio Code
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code Insiders
    • [ ] Other (please specify)

item.title: Issue: When to run, "dotnet tool update -g Microsoft.dotnet-interactive"? - @JCarnall
item.description:
Apologies if this is an obvious question! (I'm not a dotnet guru yet)

Should one run "dotnet tool update -g Microsoft.dotnet-interactive" post updating their .Net SDK to latest?

When I originally installed the .Net Interactive Extension for VS Code I guess the install of the Microsoft.dotnet-interactive tool was done for me behind the scenes?

item.title: Issue: Occasional "The name 'display' does not exist in the current context" - @Eddie-Hartman
item.description:
Describe the bug

I've seen this in the couple notebooks I've made so far. Sometimes it goes away.

Right now, I have two notebooks open. One has display and will display results just fine, but the other won't work at all. I'm not sure what the problem is or what to do. Any ideas?

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command.
  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version. .NET Interactive

© 2020 Microsoft Corporation

Version: 1.0.230903+1a272ef3782d6e88b6d36ebd648fb1882da0a530

Build date: 2021-06-13T03:27:15.8960287Z

  • OS
    • [X] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [ ] Chrome
    • [ ] Edge
    • [ ] Firefox
    • [ ] Safari
  • Frontend
    • [ ] Jupyter Notebook
    • [ ] Jupyter Lab
    • [ ] nteract
    • [X] Visual Studio Code
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code Insiders
    • [ ] Other (please specify)

Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

This is rest of the file. It's not meant to be too complicated yet:

item.title: Issue: VSCode Themes and html output - @pablofrommars
item.description:
Question

VS Code extensions (thanks to the Webview API?) have the ability to access the theme colours as css variables (for example: var(--vscode-editor-background)). Would it be possible to make the style sheets defining the theme variables visible to the html output formatter of cell results? It would enable users to create seamless integration of custom visualizations within vscode.

I am the developer of a dotnet data viz library (GG.NET). I would like to style the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Html.HtmlString emitted by the library and provide a better user experience when used within notebooks.

Thanks to anyone involved!

item.title: Issue: VSC 1.57.0 updates ignore nuget's disable state - @kkbruce
item.description:
Describe the bug

After updating VSC today, we found that the 1.57.0 update ignores NuGet's disable state. As a result, when restoring the Nuget packages, the 401 (Unauthorized) error of connecting Azure Artifacts is reported all the time.

Note: I don't know where to feedback on this issue, but this part will only go wrong in Notebook, so I want to feedback here first.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command.
.NET Interactive
© 2020 Microsoft Corporation
Version: 1.0.225602+88e6d92f1cf8ead1c4c0b982e67b4a63902e00e6
Build date: 2021-05-27T09:16:11.3076724Z
https://github.com/dotnet/interactive
  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version.
1.0.230701+897ec27256aa312cc872b52b261726684b29d42b
  • OS
    • [x] Windows 10
  • Browser
    • [x] Chrome
    • [x] Edge
  • Frontend
    • [x] Visual Studio Code

Screenshots

VSC Version

Version: 1.57.0 (user setup)
Commit: b4c1bd0a9b03c749ea011b06c6d2676c8091a70c
Date: 2021-06-09T17:18:42.354Z
Electron: 12.0.9
Chrome: 89.0.4389.128
Node.js: 14.16.0
V8: 8.9.255.25-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19042

I run dotnet nuget disable source xxx to disable all Azure Artifacts sources, but Notebook always connects to Azure Artifacts sources and reports a 401 (Unauthorized) error.

item.title: Issue: How to restore packages from pkgs.dev.azure.com - @kkbruce
item.description:
Describe the bug

Like https://github.com/dotnet/interactive/issues/1399#issuecomment-858303908 talk, We also have Azure DevOps solutions, but we don't know how to restore a similar (https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/dnceng/public/_packaging/dotnet-tools/nuget/v3/index.json) suite in Notebook?

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command.
.NET Interactive
© 2020 Microsoft Corporation
Version: 1.0.225602+88e6d92f1cf8ead1c4c0b982e67b4a63902e00e6
Build date: 2021-05-27T09:16:11.3076724Z
https://github.com/dotnet/interactive
  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version.
1.0.230701+897ec27256aa312cc872b52b261726684b29d42b
  • OS
    • [x] Windows 10
  • Browser
    • [x] Chrome
    • [x] Edge
  • Frontend
    • [x] Visual Studio Code

Screenshots

If restore packages from Azure Artifacts, will show 401 (Unauthorized) Error.

Example:

Installing package Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer, version *-*.............
Error: C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\5.0.300\NuGet.targets(131,5): error : �L�k���J�ӷ� https://pkgs.dev.azure.com/xxx/_packaging/xxx/nuget/v3/index.json ���A�ȯ��ޡC [C:\Users\BruceChen\AppData\Local\Temp\nuget\17224--1f65547b-77a1-42ee-b521-3d390a54364d\Project.fsproj]
C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\5.0.300\NuGet.targets(131,5): error : Response status code does not indicate success: 401 (Unauthorized). [C:\Users\BruceChen\AppData\Local\Temp\nuget\17224--1f65547b-77a1-42ee-b521-3d390a54364d\Project.fsproj]

I reference https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/artifacts/nuget/dotnet-exe?view=azure-devops, run dotnet restore --interactive is work, no 401 error.

item.title: Issue: Extension lab sample failed on "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.ExtensionLab,*-*" - @Mryam1
item.description:
Describe the bug

While running the sanddance sample I have got a failed package install in "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.ExtensionLab,-"

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

  • OS
    • [X] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [ ] Chrome
    • [ ] Edge
    • [ ] Firefox
    • [ ] Safari
  • Frontend
    • [ ] Jupyter Notebook
    • [ ] Jupyter Lab
    • [ ] nteract
    • [X] Visual Studio Code
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code Insiders
    • [ ] Other (please specify)

Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

item.title: Issue: Specify Language Version - @lightw8
item.description:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. .Net Interactive is the perfect code playground, but being tied to a single language version/compiler is limiting.

Describe the solution you'd like I would like to be able to explore new C# language features by specifying various language versions, like is possible with sharplab.io. Particularly cool would be "preview" compiler changes. Think this ask is related to, but not necessarily the same as, specifying the .Net version, which is here.

Describe alternatives you've considered Alternative is to use sharplab.io or to specify the LangVersion property in a Visual Studio/VS Code C# project.

item.title: Issue: June 2021 rough plan of work - @jonsequitur
item.description:
• [ ] Failing on #r "nuget" puts the kernel in unusable state (#1181)
  • [x] Finish JavaScript kernel client side command handling
  • [ ] Spike: Try .NET kernel
  • [x] User prompts in VS Code
  • [ ] Update dotnet-try to .NET 5
  • [ ] IntelliSense in Visual Studio Code is slow comparing to .cs files (#1391)
  • [x] Review notebook serialization (#1396)
    • [ ] Differentiate deserialization APIs for .dib versus .ipynb (#1427)
  • [ ] #1476

item.title: Issue: System.Net.Http.Json not recognised - @raghavan-mk
item.description:
Trying .net interactive via VS code extension. Writing a simple REST client, but getting the following error -

Error: (3,23): error CS0234: The type or namespace name 'Json' does not exist in the namespace 'System.Net.Http' (are you missing an assembly reference?) (14,15): error CS0103: The name 'JsonContent' does not exist in the current context

Also tried adding System.Net.Http.Json package explicitly but to no avail

Is it a bug or am i doing something wrong ?

Here's the code

using System.Net.Http; using System.Net.Http.Headers; using System.Net.Http.Json;

string baseUrl = ""; string api ="";

HttpClient client = new HttpClient { BaseAddress = new Uri(baseUrl) }; HttpRequestMessage request = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Post,api) { Content = JsonContent.Create(input) }; request.Headers.Accept.Add(new MediaTypeWithQualityHeaderValue("application/json")); try { var r = await client.SendAsync(request); Console.WriteLine(await r.Content.ReadAsStringAsync()); } catch(Exception ex) { Console.WriteLine(ex.ToString()); }

OS Windows 10 1909

.NET SDKs installed: 3.1.409 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk] 5.0.100 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk] 5.0.104 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk] 5.0.301 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk] 6.0.100-preview.4.21255.9 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]

item.title: Issue: Is it possible to work with the output of a sql cell in a c#/f# cell? - @JCarnall
item.description:
Just learning the ropes with .Net interactive notebooks and I love it. Hopefully a quick question.

Is it possible to work with the output of a sql cell in a subsequent c# or f# cell?

item.title: Issue: error CS0103: The name 'Formatter' does not exist in the current context - @praveenraghuvanshi1512
item.description:
Describe the bug

I was following the tutorial An Introduction to DataFrame and somehow the code for formatting the dataframe as table started throwing error "error CS0103: The name 'Formatter' does not exist in the current context". It used to work for me in the past, today seems something is broken.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using? There are a few ways to find this out:

.NET Interactive © 2020 Microsoft Corporation Version: 1.0.230701+897ec27256aa312cc872b52b261726684b29d42b Build date: 2021-06-09T11:13:17.2992510Z

  • OS
    • [X] Windows 10
    • [ ] macOS
    • [ ] Linux (Please specify distro)
    • [ ] iOS
    • [ ] Android
  • Browser
    • [ ] Chrome
    • [X] Edge
    • [ ] Firefox
    • [ ] Safari
  • Frontend
    • [ ] Jupyter Notebook
    • [X] Jupyter Lab
    • [ ] nteract
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code
    • [ ] Visual Studio Code Insiders
    • [ ] Other (please specify)

Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

item.title: Issue: Notebook with SQL Connection SQL Server failed - @kkbruce
item.description:
Describe the bug

Follow document https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/net-interactive-with-sql-net-notebooks-in-visual-studio-code/ step, can't connect to SQL Server.

Please complete the following:

Which version of .NET Interactive are you using?

  • In a notebook, run the #!about magic command.

.NET Interactive © 2020 Microsoft Corporation Version: 1.0.225602+88e6d92f1cf8ead1c4c0b982e67b4a63902e00e6 Build date: 2021-05-27T09:16:11.3076724Z https://github.com/dotnet/interactive

  • At the command line, run dotnet interactive --version.

1.0.230701+897ec27256aa312cc872b52b261726684b29d42b

  • OS
    • [x] Windows 10
  • Browser
    • [x] Chrome
    • [x] Edge
  • Frontend
    • [x] Visual Studio Code

Screenshots

step1

we need to run it on Intranet, so download https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer/ package, version 1.0.0-beta.21255.3

step2:

#!csharp
#i "nuget:Z:\NuGetPackate\"
#r "nuget:Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer,*-*"

Step3:

#!connect mssql -h

we can see the description of the command.

mssql
  Connects to a Microsoft SQL Server database

Usage:
  [options] #!connect mssql <connectionString>

Arguments:
  <connectionString>  The connection string used to connect to the database

Options:
  --create-dbcontext           Scaffold a DbContext in the C# kernel.
  --kernel-name <kernel-name>  The name of the subkernel to be added
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

Step4

#!connect mssql "server=xxx;database=yyy;user id=id;password=pwd;trusted_connection=False;connection timeout=1000;Application Name=test"

At this stage, there will be a mistake:

Error: System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SQLKernel' to type 'Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.ISupportNuget'.
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.SqlServer.MsSqlKernelConnection.CreateKernelAsync(MsSqlConnectionOptions options, KernelInvocationContext context)
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.CompositeKernel.<>c__DisplayClass27_0`1.<<AddKernelConnection>b__0>d.MoveNext() in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\CompositeKernel.cs:line 346
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at System.CommandLine.Invocation.CommandHandler.GetExitCodeAsync(Object value, InvocationContext context)
at System.CommandLine.Invocation.ModelBindingCommandHandler.InvokeAsync(InvocationContext context)
at System.CommandLine.Invocation.InvocationPipeline.<>c__DisplayClass4_0.<<BuildInvocationChain>b__0>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at System.CommandLine.Builder.CommandLineBuilderExtensions.<>c__DisplayClass21_0.<<UseMiddleware>b__0>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at System.CommandLine.Builder.CommandLineBuilderExtensions.<>c__DisplayClass16_0.<<UseHelp>b__0>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at System.CommandLine.Builder.CommandLineBuilderExtensions.<>c__DisplayClass25_0.<<UseTypoCorrections>b__0>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at System.CommandLine.Invocation.InvocationPipeline.InvokeAsync(IConsole console)
at System.CommandLine.Parsing.ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync(ParseResult parseResult, IConsole console)
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.Commands.DirectiveCommand.InvokeAsync(KernelInvocationContext context) in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\Commands\DirectiveCommand.cs:line 35
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.Kernel.HandleAsync(KernelCommand command, KernelInvocationContext context) in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\Kernel.cs:line 246
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.KernelCommandPipeline.<BuildPipeline>b__6_0(KernelCommand command, KernelInvocationContext context, KernelPipelineContinuation _) in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\KernelCommandPipeline.cs:line 57
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.KernelCommandPipeline.<>c__DisplayClass6_1.<<BuildPipeline>b__3>d.MoveNext() in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\KernelCommandPipeline.cs:line 73
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.CompositeKernel.LoadExtensions(KernelCommand command, KernelInvocationContext context, KernelPipelineContinuation next) in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\CompositeKernel.cs:line 149
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.KernelCommandPipeline.<>c__DisplayClass6_0.<<BuildPipeline>g__Combine|2>d.MoveNext() in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\KernelCommandPipeline.cs:line 74
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
at Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive.KernelCommandPipeline.SendAsync(KernelCommand command, KernelInvocationContext context) in D:\workspace\_work\1\s\src\Microsoft.DotNet.Interactive\KernelCommandPipeline.cs:line 48

item.title: Issue: I intend to include an python code programming environment in C# web application, could this tool help? - @laurence-lin
item.description:
I would like to provide an jupyter notebook programming environment in an web applications, which is built in MVC architecture.

I'd just found this dotnet notebook, and I found it could program C# with notebook.

Could this dotnet notebook tool program python too?

Thank you

item.title: Issue: Review notebook serialization - @colombod
item.description: Align object model for ipynb serialization with file schema here
